steps.title: Identify the population
steps.content: The town council wanted to assess the opinions of all town residents. The sample included only residents who own skateboards, so it left out every resident who does not own a skateboard.

steps.title: Identify the sampling bias
steps.content: Skateboard owners are likely to have different opinions about a skate park than the full group of town residents. A sample made entirely from that subgroup is not representative of the whole town.

steps.title: Choose the true statement
steps.content: The survey cannot prove that most town residents favor the skate park, and adding more skateboard owners would not fix the bias. The sample is biased because it is not representative of all town residents. The answer is D.
